findings.evidence: `SubgroupFiberOrbit.lean:78` gives class equality as orbit membership, and `Connected.lean:89` gives cancellativity; from `φ • e ∈ MulAction.orbit H e`, `IsCancelSMul.right_cancel` identifies the witnessing `h : H` with `φ`, so neither transitivity nor normality is used. Current signatures at `NormalSubgroupFiberQuotient.lean:304-327` include those stronger assumptions.
findings.fix: State the core lemma for any `H : Subgroup (Deck p)` under only `[IsCancelSMul (Deck p) (p ⁻¹' {b})]`; derive any covering wrapper from `[PreconnectedSpace E] (hp : IsCoveringMap p)` using `fiber_isCancelSMul`, without `IsRegular` or normality.
findings.issue: `subgroupFiberOrbitClass_smul_eq_base_iff` and its regular wrapper unnecessarily require `[MulAction.IsPretransitive ...]`, `[H.Normal]`, and for the wrapper `hreg : IsRegular p`.

summary: The added base-orbit criterion is stated at a normal/regular transitive level, but the natural theorem is the cancellative-action subgroup statement.
